This is the one where even people who hate me People like would say they say wow, that's like just there's something about the way I crack the code on tonality That just makes a massive difference and and just so you know, you know tonality is one of those things that I didn't realize myself How powerful it was and how how on target my tonality was until The meat business when I started in the meat business That was the first time I started training salespeople and I started noticing that when other people would speak They just didn't sound good. They just they sounded flat. They sounded bland They didn't seem excited about what they were selling. They didn't seem to believe in what they were selling they didn't sound certain about what they were selling and Those are all not those aren't quite one tonality But like I just noticed that when I was out in the field the impact It would have on people when I would speak a certain way And I realized very quickly that I was able to use Tonality in conjunction with body language out in the field, right? To get people to just simply open up and say sure. What do you got? Because when I was in the meat business, there was really very blunt force pitch wasn't like ellian, right? I have some extra boxes on the truck I'll give you a really good deal That was it and they would say not interested or what do you got? That was it was not interested or what do you got? Right? You go down one side on the other, right? Is that tonality allows you to transfer energy over the phone? That's what's happening the tonalities that you use it doesn't matter what they are reasonable man certainty phrasing it the cloud The point is is energy is flowing out of you over the phone line into the ear of the person and causing them to want to listen to your next syllable That's what's going on. That's marketing is making people take the next step Whatever that might be Well in this case when it comes to tonality when you that initial pop on the phone It's all about just getting people to take the next step And that is just all you're thinking about Is trying to get a positive open greeting from them. You can't think past that I don't know what's going to happen. They might qualify. There's a fundamental difference or radical difference between saying Hi, John, please. Hey, John Jordan Belfort calling from xyz. How you doing today? that's abiding by the so-called rules of tonality But that is an entirely different thing saying hey, John Jordan Belfort calling from xyz company Is a there's something in my voice. I just I wish I could explain to you differently. Because I really think that the the tonality module is so tight It's like all the basics are covered, right? It didn't go through 10 times. You could just keep going through it again and again There's some wrap around tonality To every other tonality all the tonalities have a wrap around tonality and that wrap around tonality is the tonality of utter complete unequivocal self-belief Self-belief that they should be talking to you And if they don't they're gonna miss out Yet out of their minds for not wanting to talk to you is a certain self-belief in a conviction in every single utterance That comes out of you It's a wrap around tonality that comes with all the others It's what makes scarcity not john reason for the call It's john reason for the call. You're four or five sentences in Guess what's happened? You've used almost every tonality you're going to use and also the shift from like staccato like beats to words that are spaced out Raise your voice lower your voice speed up slow down By the time you're literally a few sentences in if you've really mastered that You know pretty much everything you need to know to become a tonal wizard That's it So you have to Make the commitment. They're like, uh, what are you doing? You're interrupting someone right? You're an intrusion on their life. All right most people and this is the fact Walk around or spend at least 80 90 plus percent of their day In a disempowered state You know that you know that that in the morning I get up every single morning at 5 a.m I wake up at 5 a.m. I go to bed at midnight. All right, sometimes if I have to I mean I crash out at 11 30 But I'm up every morning religiously at 5 o'clock in the morning and then I lie in bed and I work Do you know that by 545 I am so utterly Tired That I would swear that I couldn't get out. I got to sleep eight hours more You know that? I wake up at 5. I'm like a tiger By 545 when it's now getting light. I am like I'm like what happened like what happened, right? Do you know that if I actually get out of bed and start doing something and walking around All right lift weights or play tennis or just start actively moving. I feel perfect My state changes most people move through the world In the state is if they're lying down. They're like zombified Most people you call are feeling like I felt at every morning at 5 35 And the reason I don't jump out of bed is I have a bad back So a lot of them always I wake up with my back certain a bit. I want to like ease into it Seriously, or else I jump out every morning. I gotta make my way out. All right, but I started noticing profoundly That I would get really tired Yet I couldn't fall back asleep and I get miserable. I am I'm too tired to work But not tired enough to sleep And yet if I just simply get out of bed Put my sneakers on brush my teeth put it right. I actually stopped my day By the time I make it downstairs. I feel perfect It's like what happened. I thought I was I needed sleep. I didn't Your body tricks you Why is that important for turn out? I'll tell you why Because most people walk around in that artificially negative depressed state all day long You're not not being a state of depression But they're not at the highest level moving and grooving. I promise you they're not What I taught my guys back in the day that was such a breakthrough I remember the word I said guys you're calling people they're living these hum drumming Even though they're rich, they're just living these average lives They just you need to be that pop that that welcome intrusion You want to be the welcome in the the voice that wakes them up the voice that makes them smile The energy that comes to and changes their state If they're in a neutral or disempowered state and you come in just sounding great not overly stupid I'm talking that bottle enthusiasm that edgy and also Is that wrap around tonality? of self-belief that Is infectious It impacts people it makes them want to hear more it makes them want to be in your sphere of influence It feels good to people You know when I do a live event But it's really we're getting a scene stalled here. It's really hot. So I'm dying right now. All right, but I'm still Rome Okay, anyways, here's the deal When I do a live event I make everybody stand up and I do an exercise It's a greeting exercise. They say, all right. I want everybody to walk around the room Just stand up right and just I want you to greet like 10 or 15 people just in your general area I walk around and just give the average Neutral green say hi, my name is so and so nice to meet you. Hi nice to be a nice Just a neutral average green the way you would typically do it right I let them go for like 30 seconds doing that. I say right guys stop Everyone stops said all right now I want you to Do the same thing Except I want you to greet someone As if you couldn't care less about them Like you're totally disinterested All right, so and by the way, I'll say and what I'll say to this they say guys, how did that? Did I tell them to do the neutral one? I had that feeling I was like, oh, it was all right. Whatever. It just seemed okay, right? I say now I want you to do it differently. I want you to say the same words But I want you to use dismissive body language. How you doing? Okay? I was all right. I said stop I said, let me ask you a question How many of you felt better? giving that greeting No one raises hand. I'm I'm using calendar for calendar right, which is awesome. Good job and text messages text message reminders There's a fine line between pestering them to reschedule and making sure they stay interested in a bible any ideas harry Um I'm gonna cover this in the follow-up right but here's here's here's some food for thought I struggled with this in the mortgage industry, right? Of course, you know, we're using a two-call system where we'd set phone appointments and follow-up in person That was we had other divisions doing just phone, but that was a big part You set up points by phone file in person and the question is is do you call to confirm? Or do you just show up? Well, what I would find just you know my my two cents here one side of the equation harry Is that if you have an appointment with someone? And you don't Confirm it and you show up and they're not there That really sucks or doesn't That person feels so bad When they find out you are at their home or their business That your closing rate for that person goes flying up I've tested this okay See when you call someone back right before to confirm You know you get this people say oh no, no, I can't you get it So there's and then there's a flip side though. Do you want to go 300 miles away? Oh, two miles and not confirm so you got to be smart about it. There is an equation here. It's not is an equation about a do I confirm an appointment And the equation is based on how far away is the appointment? How many other points do I have in the area if that appointment cancels? And then testing this idea for those because there's going to be a lot of cancellations. It won't be there But watch what happens when you show up there and you leave your card or whatever and they found out they feel so terrible That your closing rate goes up by at least 50% of better So you want to test that out test confirming versus not confirming? Okay, one of the big things I always do is you know It's like, you know, I never would say to someone Oh, I'm gonna um, oh you're over brook is great. I'm happy to come take a special trip to brookland to see you And discuss this in more detail I'm like, oh you're over brook. Oh, I'm gonna be in the area next week on tuesday and wednesday Happen or are you gonna be in the area? Which day is better for you? In other words, when I set my appointments I oh, I always see this is a whole different thing. We're gonna go for appointment setting, right? But the point is is that you're gonna get cancellations, all right and Mistake that people make is you if you try too hard to confirm and lock it in You don't give yourself that chance of them not being there And then them definitely buying on the next round you gotta test this harry, okay? And because if because if you find out that By not confirming Your cancellation rate goes up 20% but your closure goes up 50% in the back end It'll be the best move you ever made you have to test out the different permutations of this, right? Good question from chris powell when I when I call I leave a message with each call At best it takes me 2.5 minutes per call the best I've been able to do is 24 calls an hour like this. Should I not leave a message? Well, I love that. That's a great answer. Well, here's the question. How many people call back? You left something out buddy you leave messages, right? How many call back? Oh, no one ever calls you back. Well, stop leaving messages, right? If 20 with 10 call you back. How many people call back? And of those people who call back how many actually buy from you I need to know that that is all but it's all in the follow-up section It's a really big section of the straight line of how you really all these things. They're they're all like equations They're all based on mathematics and tracking and monitoring You have to be how do you what's the question? Well, you know Here's another one. Okay.
